content.problemStatement:
Sonya has NNN segments on the xxx-axis. For each valid iii, the iii-th segment contains all points between lil_ili‚Äã and rir_iri‚Äã inclusive.

Maks gave Sonya a magic number KKK. Sonya wants to choose exactly KKK of her segments in such a way that the intersection of the chosen segments is empty, i.e. there is no point which is contained in each of the chosen segments. Find the number of ways in which Sonya can choose KKK segments. Since this number can be large, Sonya only needs to know its remainder modulo 100000000710000000071000000007 (109+710^9+7109+7).
constraints: 1‚â§li‚â§ri‚â§1091 \le l_i \le r_i \le 10^91‚â§li‚Äã‚â§ri‚Äã‚â§109 for each valid iii
the sum of NNN for all test cases does not exceed 4‚ãÖ1054\cdot10^54‚ãÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers lil_ili‚Äã and rir_iri‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of ways to choose the segments, modulo 100000000710000000071000000007 (109+710^9+7109+7).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We are looking for pairs of segments. The four pairs with empty intersections are (1,3)(1,3)(1,3), (1,5)(1,5)(1,5), (2,5)(2,5)(2,5) and (4,5)(4,5)(4,5).
